mapping.a_element: N×N matrix field theory formulation of RNA folding
mapping.b_element: matrix model formulation for RNA contact structures

mapping.a_element: partition function terms classified by topological character
mapping.b_element: enumeration of RNA structures by genus (topological character of pseudoknots)

mapping.a_element: limit N→∞ yielding secondary structure (Hartree theory)
mapping.b_element: simplified case of RNA with infinitely flexible backbone (no base-pairing restrictions)

mapping.a_element: 1/N² corrections accounting for tertiary structure and pseudoknots
mapping.b_element: genus distribution of pseudoknots as a function of nucleotide count

mapping.a_element: Hartree recursion relation for secondary structure
mapping.b_element: exact enumeration results for RNA contact structures

brief_justification: Both papers employ a matrix model framework to analyze RNA folding, where the topological classification of structures (via genus or 1/N² corrections) and the partition function's role in enumerating configurations are fundamentally the same. The machinery of matrix field theory and its topological expansion drives both approaches.
